commands.review_summary:
The PR switches session-history HTTP auth and SSE reauthorization to the OpenAI-compatible scope resolver and updates the related session-history tests/mocks for bearer API-key reads.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows shared-secret bearer history requests reach `resolveTrustedHttpOperatorScopes`, receive no trusted scopes, and fail the `chat.history` `operator.read` check; I did not execute a live repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: External PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the auth/data-access boundary; this is not a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: WIP(gateway): allow bearer-auth session history reads
target.author: medns

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: medns
commands.command: re-review. i have included real behavior proof from the local development gateway below. start gateway locally: `$env:openclaw_skip_channels="1"; $env:openclaw_gateway_token="test-token-123"; $env:openclaw_gateway_auth_mode="token"; node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway` then execute http request using the bearer token: `node -e "fetch('http://127.0.0.1:19001/sessions/agent:main:main/history?limit=1', {headers: {authorization: 'bearer test-token-123'}}).then(res => res.text()).then(console.log)"` console output: ```json {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"session not found: agent:main:main"}} ``` the gateway successfully authenticates the bearer token and returns a valid 404 (session not found) instead of the previous 403 (missing scope: operator.read), proving the endpoint now correctly authorizes the caller via openai-compatible operator scopes.
commands.freeform_prompt: re-review. I have included real behavior proof from the local development gateway below. Start gateway locally: `$env:OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS="1"; $env:OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN="test-token-123"; $env:OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_AUTH_MODE="token"; node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway` Then execute HTTP request using the bearer token: `node -e "fetch('http://127.0.0.1:19001/sessions/agent:main:main/history?limit=1', {headers: {Authorization: 'Bearer test-token-123'}}).then(res => res.text()).then(console.log)"` Console output: ```json {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Session not found: agent:main:main"}} ``` The gateway successfully authenticates the bearer token and returns a valid 404 (session not found) instead of the previous 403 (missing scope: operator.read), proving the endpoint now correctly authorizes the caller via OpenAI-compatible operator scopes.
